verdict.answer_first: A leads harder: "Plain Python." B also answers first: "default to plain Python," but softer.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more cleanly with "Plain Python" and a single rule: "for each row, do X"; B adds more branches after the same default.
verdict.fabrication: both add unverified thresholds/timings: A "under ~10k rows" and "multi-second import"; B "few thousand rows" and "~0.5–1s".
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it includes a working csv loop: "csv.DictReader" and "DictWriter".
